name: Edge Cases
description: Questions on empty inputs, boundaries, and defensive checks.

questions.question: max([]) in Python raises:
questions.explanation: max() of an empty sequence raises ValueError; there is no maximum.

questions.question: For a function that takes a list, an important edge case is:
questions.explanation: Empty list often needs special handling (e.g. return 0, None, or []) to avoid index errors.

questions.question: Division by zero in Python raises:
Checking for None before using an optional value is an example of:
questions.explanation: Handling None (and empty/zero) avoids AttributeError and logical bugs.

questions.question: lst[0] when lst is [] raises:
questions.explanation: Indexing an empty list (or out-of-range index) raises IndexError.
